heroText: Munich is a key city in Germany, combining landmarks, local culture, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Munich
highlights.text: Munich is a key city in Germany, combining landmarks, local culture, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: 11th century
history.text: Archaeological excavations at Marienhof Square (near Marienplatz) in advance of the expansion of the S-Bahn (subway) in 2012 discovered shards of vessels from the 11th century, which prove again that the settlement of Munich.

In 1180, after Henry the Lion's fall from grace with Emperor Frederick Barbarossa, including his trial and exile, Otto I Wittelsbach became Duke of Bavaria, and Munich was handed to the Bishop of Freising.
During its time as the capital of the Electorate of Bavaria, Munich was an important centre of Baroque life, but also had to suffer under Habsburg occupations in 1704 after Bavaria's defeat in the.

history.label: 1790s
history.text: In the 1790s, Munich became the largest German city to remove its fortifications, starting in 1791 under Karl Theodor and Rumford.
After making an alliance with Napoleonic France, the city became the capital of the new Kingdom of Bavaria in 1806 with Elector Maximilian IV Joseph becoming its first king.
The first Munich railway station was built in 1839, with a line going to Augsburg in the west.
By the time Ludwig II became king in 1864, he remained mostly aloof from his capital and focused more on his fanciful castles in the Bavarian countryside.
Its founding members include Gabriele Münter. World War I to World War II Following the outbreak of World War I in 1914, life in Munich became very difficult, as the Allied blockade of Germany.
The public limited company BMW AG was founded in 1918, with Camillo Castiglioni owning one third of the share capital.
Munich again became important to the Nazis when they took power in Germany in 1933.
The Olympic Park subway station was built near the BMW Headquarters and the line was completed in May 1972, three months before the opening of the 1972 Summer Olympics.
The Munich Airport, which commenced operations in 1992, was named in his honor.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Henry built a new toll bridge, customs house and a coin market closer to his home, somewhat upstream, at a settlement around the area of modern old town Munich.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: After US occupation Munich was completely rebuilt following a meticulous plan, which preserved its pre-war street grid, bar a few exceptions owing to then-modern traffic concepts.
